guest: Will O'Brien, CEO of Ulyses (ocean robotics)

business_ideas.idea: Autonomous underwater robots for seagrass restoration and ocean tasks
Unmanned surface vehicles for ocean defense and surveillance
Data centers submerged in the ocean for AI cooling efficiency
Subsea cable protection service using autonomous drones
Ocean treasure hunting and salvage with profit-sharing agreements
Marine geoengineering platform for carbon sequestration and ecosystem management
Cultivated/lab-grown sushi-grade salmon for sustainable seafood
Cloud seeding and weather manipulation via atmospheric iron deployment

predictions.text: A billion-dollar company will be built in marine geoengineering and ocean stewardship
New generation of tech founders combining physical fitness, entrepreneurial ambition, tech skills will dominate 2020s
The ocean economy will see a wave of disruption similar to SpaceX/Blue Origin in space

dynamics: Shaan opens enthusiastically about ocean opportunities; Sam expresses fear of ocean but intellectual fascination. Will drives with domain expertise and contagious passion. Episode builds momentum toward shared conviction about new generation of unconventional founders embodying 'tech bro' identity proudly. Tone shifts from skeptical education to inspirational by end.
frameworks: Technology Windows (James Krueger) — defined periods where tech enablers create brief startup opportunity windows before incumbents dominate
Ocean Economy Tripartite Model — segments ocean business into biosphere, prosperity-oriented, and defense/security
Hobbyist-to-Billionaire Arc — status moment, knowledge diffusion, competition, incumbent regime
learnings: Low-hanging software fruit has been consumed; next frontier is hardware-intensive problems requiring domain expertise and contrarian thinking
Coming from outside an industry can be an advantage when disrupting it; prior assumptions from incumbents limit innovation
Conspiratorial/contrarian mindset shares traits with great founders—pattern recognition, willingness to pursue unusual ideas despite skepticism

banger_quotes.quote: Give me a tanker of iron filings and I will give you an ice age.
I've been put on this planet to protect wildlife. Every single cent goes straight into conservation.
To really shake up an industry, it's probably good if you don't come from it.

numbers_dropped: Ocean coverage: 70% of planet, only 25% explored
Ocean food/income: 3 billion rely on ocean for diet, 1 billion for primary income
Baltic Sea cable cuts: 11 in past year
notable: Guest embodies new archetype of tech founder—combines physical fitness, cowboy aesthetic, contrarian thinking, domain passion with Silicon Valley ambition; represents broader cultural shift in tech beyond pure software.
